description: The Borel exclusion argument checks that h1 has nonzero (2,1) entry in the standard basis, but a matrix may lie in a Borel subgroup after conjugation. Must show H fixes no 1-dimensional subspace of F_p^2, not just check one basis.
action_taken: Proof rewritten using non-circular Dickson classification. Borel: invariant-subspace analysis (eigenvectors of g1 and g2 provably distinct for p>=7). Cartan: eigenline pair analysis (4 distinct lines cannot be preserved). Exceptional: generator order exceeds 60 for p>=61. Small primes p<61 verified by BFS.
